1.name: hello
1.hand: Open hand with fingers together, palm facing outward. Fingertips touch the forehead or temple and the hand moves outward and slightly downward away from the head in a saluting gesture.
1.face: Neutral or pleasant expression. Eyebrows in a relaxed, neutral position. Slight smile is natural and acceptable.
1.body: Torso upright and facing forward. Dominant arm raised with elbow bent, hand at head height. Shoulder relaxed.

2.name: thank you
2.hand: Open flat hand with fingers together, palm facing inward toward the signer. Fingertips touch the chin or lips and the hand moves outward and slightly downward away from the face.
2.face: Warm, grateful expression. Eyebrows relaxed or slightly raised. Slight smile or neutral mouth position.
2.body: Torso upright. Dominant arm extends forward from chin level. Movement is smooth and deliberate, not rushed.

3.name: yes
3.hand: Dominant hand forms a fist with the index finger extended (or full fist). The hand nods up and down at the wrist, mimicking a head nodding yes.
3.face: Affirmative expression. Eyebrows may be slightly raised for emphasis. Mouth closed or in a slight smile.
3.body: Torso upright or with a very slight forward lean. Head may nod slightly in coordination with the hand movement.

4.name: no
4.hand: Dominant hand with index and middle fingers extended (or index finger only). The hand shakes side to side at the wrist, mimicking a head shaking no.
4.face: Negative or neutral expression. Eyebrows may be furrowed slightly. Lips may press together.
4.body: Torso upright. Head may shake slightly in coordination with the hand movement. Shoulders relaxed.

5.name: please
5.hand: Open flat hand placed on the chest with palm facing inward. Hand makes a circular rubbing motion on the chest in a clockwise direction.
5.face: Polite, earnest expression. Eyebrows in a neutral or slightly raised position. Mouth relaxed or in a slight smile.
5.body: Torso upright and facing forward. Dominant arm bent with hand on chest. Posture should convey sincerity.

6.name: sorry
6.hand: Dominant hand forms a fist. The fist is placed on the chest and makes a circular rubbing motion, similar to please but with a closed fist instead of open palm.
6.face: Apologetic or remorseful expression. Eyebrows slightly furrowed or raised in concern. Lips may press together or mouth corners turn slightly down.
6.body: Torso may have a very slight forward lean to convey sincerity. Head may tilt slightly downward. Shoulders relaxed.

7.name: water
7.hand: Dominant hand forms the letter W (three middle fingers extended, index and pinky bent). The index finger side of the hand taps against the chin twice.
7.face: Neutral expression. Eyebrows in a relaxed position. Mouth may open slightly as if about to drink.
7.body: Torso upright and facing forward. Dominant arm bent with hand at chin height. Movement is small and tapping, not sweeping.

8.name: help
8.hand: Dominant fist placed on top of the open non-dominant palm (thumbs-up fist on flat palm). Both hands move upward together from waist or chest height.
8.face: Expression should be neutral or slightly concerned. Eyebrows in a neutral position for a statement. If asking for help as a question, eyebrows must be raised.
8.body: Torso upright and facing forward. Slight forward lean is acceptable. Arms extended in front of the body at chest height, both hands visible.

9.name: name
9.hand: Both hands form the letter H (index and middle fingers extended horizontally). The dominant H-hand taps down onto the non-dominant H-hand twice in a crossing motion.
9.face: Neutral or curious expression. Eyebrows relaxed. Mouth closed.
9.body: Torso upright. Both arms extended in front of the body at chest or waist height. Movement is small and tapping downward.

10.name: are you hungry?
10.hand: Dominant hand forms the letter C (curved fingers and thumb forming a C shape). The C-hand moves downward along the chest from throat to stomach, tracing the esophagus.
10.face: CRITICAL: Eyebrows MUST be raised for this yes/no question. This is grammatically required in ASL — raised eyebrows signal a yes/no question. Mouth may be slightly open. Head may tilt slightly forward.
10.body: Torso upright or with a slight forward lean toward the conversation partner, which is natural when asking a question. Dominant arm bent with hand at upper chest/throat level.
